Title: Overexpression of Arabidopsis nucleolar GTP-binding 1 (NOG1) proteins confers drought tolerance in rice

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Functional divergence between NOG1 paralogs is suggested in rice: AtNOG1-1 overexpression has a stronger effect on increasing cell sap osmolality than AtNOG1-2, indicating specialization towards osmotic adjustment in the heterologous monocot system.
Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. AtNOG1-1, a small nucleolar GTP-binding protein from Arabidopsis, when constitutively expressed in rice, confers robust drought tolerance at vegetative and reproductive stages.
2. AtNOG1-1-OX rice plants maintain or increase shoot biomass and grain yield under drought without yield penalty under well-watered conditions.
3. Overexpression of AtNOG1-1 increases cell sap osmolality, relative water content, and ABA levels while decreasing leaf water loss during drought.
4. AtNOG1-1 promotes formation and tighter packing of silica bodies around stomata, providing a secondary barrier to transpirational water loss.
5. Transcriptome analysis shows that AtNOG1-1 modulates expression of core sets of rice genes involved in ABA signaling, JA metabolism, stomatal regulation, osmotic potential, and stress protection.
Plant_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq transcriptome profiling of AtNOG1-1-OX, AtNOG1-2-OX, OsNOG1-RNAi, and empty-vector control rice lines under drought and well-watered conditions; data deposited under NCBI BioProject accession PRJNA805197.
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Constitutive overexpression of AtNOG1-1 in rice enhances drought tolerance at both vegetative and reproductive stages without yield penalty, increases shoot biomass and seed yield under drought, elevates cell sap osmolality, relative water content, and ABA levels in leaves, and reduces leaf water loss, partly via promoting silica body formation around stomata that restricts transpirational water loss.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: Nuclear-localized small GTPase that, when overexpressed, alters transcript levels of multiple ABA- and JA-related, stomatal, ion transport, and stress-protection genes, and promotes formation and dense arrangement of silica bodies around stomata to reduce transpirational water loss.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Pathway: Acts in drought stress-response pathways including ABA signaling, jasmonic acid metabolism, stomatal regulation, osmotic potential maintenance, and regulation of silica body formation around stomata.
Plant_Genes.Upstream_or_Downstream_Regulation: Overexpression of AtNOG1-1 in rice positively regulates the expression of a core set of drought- and hormone-related rice genes, including ABA- and brassinosteroid-related genes (e.g., LOC_Os08g44590/GAS2, LOC_Os04g54900/bHLH protein, LOC_Os12g35610/NADPH oxidase, LOC_Os11g31530/SERK family RLK, LOC_Os03g58750/LRK10L-1.5, LOC_Os11g04960/ANAC029), leaf-surface and trichome/silica body-related genes (LOC_Os01g72530, LOC_Os06g46950), ion transporter LOC_Os03g37830 (HAK5), and other drought/stress genes, as inferred from RNA-seq comparison of AtNOG1-1-OX, AtNOG1-2-OX, and OsNOG1-RNAi lines under drought.
Plant_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: AtNOG1-1-GFP localizes to the nucleus and plasma membrane in rice seedling cells, as shown by transient expression and fluorescence microscopy.
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: AtNOG1-1 transcripts are strongly accumulated in AtNOG1-1-OX rice lines relative to empty-vector control, whereas endogenous OsNOG1 transcript levels are not significantly altered in these overexpressors.
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Drought stress imposed by withholding watering on potted rice plants at vegetative (45–56 days old) and reproductive (heading, 85 days old) stages until soil moisture dropped to 0%, followed by re-watering for recovery; mild cyclic drought during reproductive stage for yield analysis; comparison with continuously well-watered controls.
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Drought tolerance, stomatal regulation, silica body formation around stomata, and maintenance of yield under drought via transgenic expression of small GTPase NOG1-1.
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: Abiotic stress tolerance (drought tolerance), grain yield under drought, stomatal transpiration control, and osmotic adjustment.
Plant_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: Under drought, AtNOG1-1-OX plants survive better than control after 11 days without water at vegetative and reproductive stages and recover with higher biomass; shoot biomass and seed weight per plant are significantly increased under mild drought compared with control; cell sap osmolality and relative water content are significantly higher and leaf water loss significantly lower in AtNOG1-1-OX lines than in control; ABA content in drought-stressed leaves is significantly elevated (statistical significance determined by ANOVA or t test).
Plant_Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: AtNOG1-1-OX lines show shoot biomass and seed weight comparable to control under well-watered conditions, indicating no noticeable growth or yield penalty from overexpression.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: Transgenic rice (Oryza sativa japonica cv. Nipponbare) lines AtNOG1-1-OX (T2/T3 generations) carrying CaMV 35S::AtNOG1-1 in the pMDC32 vector; empty-vector (pMDC32) control plants.
Plant_Genes.Variety: Nipponbare (Oryza sativa japonica cultivar used as heterologous host)
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: Agrobacterium-mediated transformation of rice, generation of stable transgenic lines, RT-qPCR to quantify AtNOG1-1 and OsNOG1 transcripts, western blotting for NOG1 protein, drought imposition by withholding water at vegetative and reproductive stages, recovery assays, measurement of shoot biomass and seed yield, cell sap osmolality, relative water content, leaf water loss, ABA quantification by UPLC-MS, scanning electron microscopy to analyze silica bodies around stomata, and RNA-seq transcriptome profiling.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: AtNOG1-1 coding sequence was PCR-amplified from Arabidopsis, cloned into pDONR207 and subsequently into the binary overexpression vector pMDC32 under a CaMV 35S promoter, followed by Agrobacterium-mediated transformation into rice.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: AtNOG1-1 is a promising transgene for engineering drought-tolerant rice and potentially other cereals, as its constitutive overexpression confers strong drought tolerance at both vegetative and reproductive stages, increases biomass and grain yield under drought, and does not penalize yield under well-watered conditions.

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Functional divergence between NOG1 paralogs in the rice system is indicated: AtNOG1-2 overexpression has a stronger influence on silica body formation and stomatal coverage than AtNOG1-1, suggesting specialization towards structural protection of stomata during drought.
Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. AtNOG1-2, a small nucleolar GTP-binding protein from Arabidopsis, when constitutively expressed in rice, confers strong drought tolerance at vegetative and reproductive stages.
2. AtNOG1-2-OX rice plants show increased shoot biomass and grain yield under drought, without yield penalties under well-watered conditions.
3. Overexpression of AtNOG1-2 elevates cell sap osmolality, relative water content, and ABA levels and reduces leaf water loss during drought.
4. AtNOG1-2 has a predominant role in promoting elongation and increased number of silica bodies surrounding stomata, reducing the area available for stomatal transpiration.
5. Transcriptome analysis shows AtNOG1-2 regulates overlapping and distinct sets of rice genes compared with AtNOG1-1, including many involved in ABA and JA signaling, stomatal regulation, osmotic potential, and stress protection, indicating partial functional divergence between the paralogs.
Plant_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq transcriptome profiling of AtNOG1-2-OX, AtNOG1-1-OX, OsNOG1-RNAi, and control rice under drought and well-watered conditions; NCBI BioProject PRJNA805197.
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Constitutive overexpression of AtNOG1-2 in rice confers enhanced drought tolerance at vegetative and reproductive stages without yield penalty, increases shoot biomass and seed yield under drought, elevates cell sap osmolality, relative water content, and ABA levels in leaves, and reduces leaf water loss, with a particularly strong effect on silica body formation around stomata that limits transpirational water loss.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: Nuclear-localized small GTPase that, when overexpressed, alters expression of multiple ABA/JA, stomatal, ion transport, and stress-response genes and strongly promotes formation and dense arrangement of silica bodies surrounding stomata to reduce transpirational water loss.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Pathway: Functions in drought stress-response pathways including ABA signaling, jasmonic acid metabolism, stomatal regulation, osmotic potential maintenance, and silica body formation around stomata.
Plant_Genes.Upstream_or_Downstream_Regulation: Overexpression of AtNOG1-2 in rice positively regulates core drought- and hormone-related genes similarly to AtNOG1-1, including ABA/GA and brassinosteroid components (LOC_Os08g44590, LOC_Os04g54900, LOC_Os12g35610, LOC_Os11g31530, LOC_Os03g58750, LOC_Os11g04960), leaf surface and trichome-related genes linked to silica body formation (LOC_Os01g72530, LOC_Os06g46950), potassium transporter LOC_Os03g37830, and other stress-related genes, as inferred from RNA-seq and comparison with OsNOG1-RNAi lines.
Plant_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: AtNOG1-2-GFP localizes to the nucleus and plasma membrane in rice seedling cells, as shown by transient expression and fluorescence microscopy.
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: AtNOG1-2 transcripts are strongly accumulated in AtNOG1-2-OX rice lines relative to empty-vector control, whereas endogenous OsNOG1 transcript levels are not significantly altered in these overexpressors.
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Drought stress imposed by withholding watering on potted rice plants at vegetative and reproductive stages until soil moisture reached 0%, followed by re-watering, as well as mild cyclic drought during reproductive stage for yield analysis, compared with continuously well-watered controls.
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Drought tolerance, stomatal regulation, silica body formation around stomata, and maintenance of yield under drought via transgenic expression of small GTPase NOG1-2.
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: Abiotic stress tolerance (drought tolerance), grain yield under drought, stomatal transpiration control, and osmotic adjustment.
Plant_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: Under drought, AtNOG1-2-OX plants are greener and survive better than control after 11 days without watering at both vegetative and reproductive stages and recover with higher biomass; shoot biomass and seed weight per plant are significantly increased under mild drought compared with control; cell sap osmolality, relative water content, and leaf ABA content are significantly higher, and leaf water loss significantly lower, than in control plants.
Plant_Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: AtNOG1-2-OX lines maintain shoot biomass and seed weight comparable to control under well-watered conditions, indicating no apparent growth or yield penalty; under drought they tend to show particularly dense silica body coverage of stomata.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: Transgenic rice (Oryza sativa japonica cv. Nipponbare) lines AtNOG1-2-OX (T2/T3 generations) carrying CaMV 35S::AtNOG1-2 in the pMDC32 vector; empty-vector (pMDC32) control plants.
Plant_Genes.Variety: Nipponbare (Oryza sativa japonica cultivar used as heterologous host)
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: Agrobacterium-mediated transformation of rice, generation of stable AtNOG1-2-OX lines, RT-qPCR to quantify AtNOG1-2 and OsNOG1 transcripts, western blotting for NOG1 protein, drought imposition and recovery assays, measurement of shoot biomass and seed yield, physiological measurements (cell sap osmolality, relative water content, leaf water loss), ABA quantification by UPLC-MS, scanning electron microscopy of stomata and silica bodies, and RNA-seq transcriptome profiling.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: AtNOG1-2 coding sequence was PCR-amplified from Arabidopsis, cloned into pDONR207 and then into the CaMV 35S-driven overexpression vector pMDC32, followed by Agrobacterium-mediated transformation into rice.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: AtNOG1-2 is a strong candidate gene for transgenic improvement of drought tolerance in rice and other cereals because its overexpression markedly enhances drought tolerance, promotes protective silica body coverage around stomata, and increases biomass and grain yield under drought without compromising performance under well-watered conditions.

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Rice carries two nearly identical OsNOG1 paralogs (LOC_Os06g09570 and LOC_Os07g01920) that are 96% identical at the coding sequence and 98% at the protein level; the RNAi construct targets both paralogs, so the OsNOG1-RNAi phenotype reflects combined knockdown of both genes.
Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. OsNOG1, comprising two highly similar paralogs in rice, encodes a small nucleolar GTP-binding protein required for drought tolerance and normal growth.
2. RNAi-mediated knockdown of OsNOG1 reduces transcript and protein levels and leads to rapid wilting, poor survival, and low recovery under drought at vegetative and reproductive stages.
3. OsNOG1-RNAi lines have reduced shoot biomass and seed yield under both well-watered and drought conditions.
4. Physiologically, OsNOG1 knockdown decreases cell sap osmolality, relative water content, and ABA levels while increasing leaf water loss.
5. OsNOG1 is necessary for proper formation and tight arrangement of silica bodies around stomata; OsNOG1-RNAi plants have smaller, fewer, and more widely spaced silica bodies, increasing the area for stomatal transpiration.
6. Transcriptome analysis shows that OsNOG1 positively regulates many drought-, ABA-, JA-, stomatal-, ion transport-, antioxidant-, and growth-related genes, and its loss leads to downregulation of core drought-induced genes and upregulation of stress-sensitive gene sets.
Plant_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq transcriptome profiling of OsNOG1-RNAi, AtNOG1-1-OX, AtNOG1-2-OX, and control rice lines under drought and well-watered conditions to identify OsNOG1-regulated gene sets; data available under NCBI BioProject PRJNA805197.
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Downregulation of OsNOG1 in rice leads to enhanced drought susceptibility at vegetative and reproductive stages, with plants wilting and dying faster than control under drought, poor recovery after re-watering, and significantly reduced shoot biomass and seed yield under both well-watered and drought conditions; OsNOG1-RNAi lines show lower cell sap osmolality, lower relative water content, and reduced ABA levels under drought, but higher leaf water loss and diminished silica body size, number, and density around stomata.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: Endogenous OsNOG1 functions as a positive regulator of drought tolerance and growth: its knockdown decreases expression of many ABA- and JA-related, stomatal, antioxidant, and drought-responsive genes, reduces silica body formation and packing around stomata, and thereby increases transpirational water loss and drought susceptibility.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Pathway: OsNOG1 acts in drought stress-response pathways, including ABA signaling, jasmonic acid metabolism, stomatal regulation, osmotic potential maintenance, silica body formation around stomata, oxidative stress protection, and general drought tolerance.
Plant_Genes.Upstream_or_Downstream_Regulation: Silencing OsNOG1 in rice (OsNOG1-RNAi) causes downregulation of 41 drought-induced genes and numerous additional stress- and growth-related genes compared with control, including: LOC_Os06g11280 (OPR2, JA biosynthesis), several CYP94 family members involved in JA-Ile oxidation, ABA- and stomatal-related genes such as LOC_Os08g44590 (GAS2), LOC_Os04g54900 (bHLH protein), LOC_Os12g35610 (NADPH oxidase), LOC_Os11g31530 (SERK RLK), LOC_Os03g58750 (LRK10L-1.5), LOC_Os11g04960 (ANAC029), leaf-surface and trichome/silica body-related genes LOC_Os01g72530 and LOC_Os06g46950, high-affinity potassium transporter LOC_Os03g37830 (HAK5), root hair and auxin-homeostasis genes LOC_Os05g49240 and LOC_Os04g39980, antioxidant and disease-resistance genes LOC_Os01g45110, LOC_Os09g25070, LOC_Os04g58810, and several glutathione-S-transferases, indicating that OsNOG1 positively regulates these pathways at the transcript level.
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: RT-qPCR shows that OsNOG1 transcripts are reduced approximately two- to five-fold in OsNOG1-RNAi lines compared with control plants, while western blotting detects a marked decrease of the 77-kDa NOG1 protein band in OsNOG1-RNAi lines relative to control.
Plant_Genes.Reference_Genome_Version: Oryza sativa japonica reference genome MSU release 7
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Drought stress induced by withholding water from potted rice plants at vegetative and reproductive stages until soil moisture reached 0%, followed by re-watering for recovery; mild cyclical drought imposed at reproductive stage for yield analysis; comparisons with continuously well-watered plants.
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Genetic regulation of drought tolerance, stomatal function and silica body formation, osmotic adjustment, growth and yield in rice via the endogenous small GTPase OsNOG1.
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: Abiotic stress tolerance (drought tolerance and susceptibility), grain yield under drought and well-watered conditions, stomatal transpiration control, osmotic adjustment, and general plant growth.
Plant_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: Under well-watered conditions, OsNOG1-RNAi plants show significantly lower shoot biomass and seed weight than control; under drought, they dry faster, have markedly reduced survival after 11 days without water, and show significantly reduced shoot biomass and seed yield under mild drought. Physiological measurements indicate that OsNOG1-RNAi leaves have significantly lower cell sap osmolality and relative water content but higher leaf water loss compared with control, and ABA content in drought-stressed leaves is significantly reduced. Silica bodies around stomata are significantly shorter, fewer, and more widely spaced than in control plants.
Plant_Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: OsNOG1-RNAi lines exhibit reduced shoot biomass and seed weight compared with control plants even under well-watered conditions, indicating a role for OsNOG1 in normal growth and yield in addition to drought responses.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: Transgenic OsNOG1-RNAi rice lines (japonica cv. Nipponbare) in which both OsNOG1 paralogs (LOC_Os06g09570 and LOC_Os07g01920) are downregulated; empty-vector (pMDC32) transformed control plants.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: Agrobacterium-mediated transformation to generate OsNOG1-RNAi lines, RT-qPCR to quantify OsNOG1 transcripts, western blotting with anti-GTPBP4 antibody to detect NOG1 protein, drought imposition and recovery assays at vegetative and reproductive stages, measurement of shoot biomass and seed yield, cell sap osmolality, relative water content, leaf water loss, ABA quantification by UPLC-MS, scanning electron microscopy of stomata and silica bodies, and RNA-seq transcriptome profiling comparing OsNOG1-RNAi, AtNOG1-OX, and control lines.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Two OsNOG1 paralogs, highly similar homologs of Arabidopsis NOG1, were identified in rice (LOC_Os06g09570 and LOC_Os07g01920) by phylogenetic analysis. For functional analysis, a 282-nt OsNOG1 fragment was PCR-amplified and cloned into the RNAi vector pH7GWIWG2, and the construct was introduced into rice by Agrobacterium-mediated transformation to generate OsNOG1-RNAi lines targeting both paralogs.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: OsNOG1 is essential for drought tolerance and normal growth in rice; its loss-of-function via RNAi causes severe drought susceptibility and yield loss under both well-watered and drought conditions. These results indicate that maintaining or enhancing functional OsNOG1 alleles is important for breeding or biotechnological strategies aiming to improve drought tolerance and sustain yield in rice, and OsNOG1-linked markers could be used to avoid deleterious alleles.
